title: Getting Started with Next.js 14
content:
# Getting Started with Next.js 14

Next.js 14 brings exciting new features and improvements that make building modern web applications faster and more efficient.

## Why Next.js?

Next.js has become the go-to framework for React developers because it provides:

- Server-side rendering (SSR)
- Static site generation (SSG)
- API routes for backend functionality
- Built-in optimization
- File-based routing

## Key Features

### 1. App Router
The new App Router provides a more intuitive way to organize your application structure.

### 2. Server Components
Build faster, more secure applications with Server Components that execute only on the server.

### 3. Built-in Optimizations
Automatic image optimization, font optimization, and script optimization.

## Getting Started

To create a new Next.js project, run:

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest my-app
cd my-app
npm run dev
```

Your development server will start at http://localhost:3000

## Conclusion

Next.js 14 is a powerful framework for building production-grade React applications. Whether you're building a simple blog or a complex web application, Next.js provides the tools you need to succeed.
excerpt: Learn how to get started with Next.js 14 and leverage its powerful features for building modern web applications.
author: Nana Nandintsetseg

title: CAB201 – Programming Principles
content:
# CAB201: Programming Principles – Building Strong OOP Foundations

This was one of my first deep dives into proper software engineering at QUT.

## What the unit covered
- Core OOP concepts: classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, abstraction
- Java fundamentals: collections, generics, exceptions, streams
- Design principles: SOLID, DRY, KISS
- Basic design patterns (Factory, Singleton basics)
- Unit testing with JUnit

## Key assignments & projects
I built several medium-sized console applications, including:
- A task management system with user authentication simulation
- An inventory management program using inheritance hierarchies

These forced me to think about code organisation, reusability, and testability from the beginning.

## What I learned & personal takeaways
Before this unit I wrote code that "worked". After CAB201 I started writing code that other developers (and future me) could understand and extend easily.  
Learning to apply SOLID principles changed how I approach almost every programming task now.

This unit laid the groundwork for later software development and team project units.
excerpt: CAB201 at QUT taught me the core principles of object-oriented programming in Java, focusing on clean, maintainable, and scalable code design.
author: Nana Nandintsetseg
